Best Software

Best HVAC Construction Software (2026)

HVAC contractors need both service scheduling and project management. The right software handles both sides of the business.

✓ Updated April 2026

HVAC contractors are often running two different business models at once: service calls (maintenance, repairs, emergencies) and installation projects (new systems, duct work, commercial mechanical). The software that works best for one doesn't always work for the other.

This guide covers the best tools for each scenario — and the platforms that bridge both.

Quick comparison

Software Starting Price Best For Key Strength
ServiceTitan Custom (~$400+/mo) Established HVAC service businesses Dispatching, flat-rate pricing, memberships
Jobber $69/mo Small HVAC companies, service & installs Quoting, scheduling, invoicing
Knowify ~$149/mo Commercial HVAC subcontractors AIA billing, job costing
Buildertrend $499/mo HVAC doing large residential/commercial installs Full PM, client portal, scheduling
Contractor Foreman $49/mo Budget-conscious HVAC contractors Estimates, scheduling, job costing

1. ServiceTitan — Best for HVAC service businesses

Pricing: Custom (typically $400–$600+/mo for small teams; scales up)

ServiceTitan is the dominant platform in HVAC service management. If your primary business model is service calls, maintenance agreements, and emergency repairs, no platform comes close to ServiceTitan's operational depth. It was built specifically for residential and commercial HVAC service companies.

Why HVAC service companies choose it:

  • Dispatch board: Visual, color-coded scheduling board with real-time technician GPS tracking
  • Flat-rate price book: Techs present good/better/best options on a tablet in the customer's home
  • Maintenance agreements: Track and bill recurring service memberships automatically
  • Customer history: Every past service call, installed unit, and warranty is one tap away on a tech's phone
  • Revenue tracking: Real-time revenue dashboards by technician, call type, or location
  • Financing integration: Present financing options in the field for big system replacements

Limitations:

  • Expensive — pricing isn't transparent and requires a demo
  • Complex to implement; plan for a multi-week onboarding process
  • Overkill for small HVAC companies or those doing primarily new construction work
  • Not designed for large commercial mechanical projects with AIA billing

2. Jobber — Best for small HVAC companies

Pricing: Core $69/mo · Connect $169/mo · Grow $349/mo

For small HVAC companies that don't need ServiceTitan's complexity, Jobber is the fastest way to get organized. Quotes, scheduling, crew dispatch, invoicing, and payment collection all work cleanly without a steep learning curve. If you're under 10 technicians and don't need flat-rate pricing books or maintenance agreement tracking, Jobber covers the core workflows at a fraction of ServiceTitan's cost.

Strengths for small HVAC:

  • Online quote requests and approval by customers
  • Drag-and-drop schedule with mobile app for technicians
  • Time tracking and job notes in the field
  • Automated follow-up emails and payment reminders
  • Quick to set up — many contractors are operational in a day

Limitations:

  • No maintenance membership management
  • No flat-rate pricing book
  • Not designed for complex commercial installation projects

3. Knowify — Best for commercial HVAC subcontractors

Pricing: ~$149/mo starter

If you're an HVAC mechanical subcontractor doing commercial work — bidding on GC projects, billing with AIA pay applications, managing subcontracts — Knowify is built for your workflow. Most service-oriented platforms ignore AIA billing entirely. Knowify handles G702/G703 applications for payment, change orders tracked against original contract values, and job costing against your bid.

Who this fits: Mechanical contractors doing commercial tenant improvements, new construction HVAC, or industrial HVAC installation — not primarily residential service work.

4. Buildertrend — Best for large HVAC installation projects

Pricing: Essential $499/mo · Advanced $799/mo · Complete $1,099/mo

For HVAC contractors doing large residential or light commercial installation projects as a general or primary contractor — new home HVAC systems, large commercial retrofits — Buildertrend offers project management depth that service-focused platforms don't provide. Scheduling, subcontractor coordination, change order management, and client communication all in one platform.

Buildertrend — When your HVAC projects are large enough to need real project management.

Visit Buildertrend →

5. Contractor Foreman — Best budget option

Pricing: Basic $49/mo · Standard $79/mo · Pro $159/mo

For HVAC contractors who need better tools than paper and spreadsheets but aren't ready to commit to ServiceTitan or Buildertrend pricing, Contractor Foreman provides estimating, job tracking, scheduling, time tracking, and invoicing at a low monthly cost. The interface is less polished but the feature set is comprehensive.

HVAC-specific features to look for

For service-heavy HVAC businesses

  • Maintenance agreement / membership tracking
  • Equipment tracking per customer location (unit model, install date, warranty)
  • Dispatch board with real-time tech location
  • Flat-rate pricing book with good/better/best presentation
  • Mobile app with offline capability (some job sites have no signal)
  • Customer history tied to service address

For commercial HVAC installation projects

  • Detailed job costing tied to budget line items
  • AIA billing / applications for payment
  • Change order management with GC approval workflow
  • Subcontractor management (if you use duct fabrication subs, etc.)
  • Lien waiver tracking
  • Equipment delivery and scheduling coordination

Making the decision

Primarily residential service calls: Jobber (small) or ServiceTitan (scaling up)

Commercial mechanical subcontracting: Knowify

Large installation projects with PM needs: Buildertrend or Contractor Foreman

Budget is the primary constraint: Contractor Foreman at $49/mo
